From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Musical Instrument

The **bowed dulcimer** is a musical instrument. Designed in the style of the [Appalachian dulcimer](/wiki/Appalachian_dulcimer "Appalachian dulcimer") (a fretted string instrument of the [zither](/wiki/Zither "Zither") family, typically with three or four strings), it is either a standard instrument played with a [violin bow](/wiki/Violin_bow "Violin bow"), or a purpose-built dulcimer designed around bow playing. The purpose-designed instrument is described as resembling a hybrid between a dulcimer and a cello or [viola da gamba](/wiki/Viola_da_gamba "Viola da gamba").[[1]](#cite_note-BarrettStauffer2012-1)

Bowing as a technique of playing the standard dulcimer has some historical roots;[[2]](#cite_note-Irwin1983-2) L. Allen Smith feature several examples in his historical survey *A Catalogue of Pre-Revival Appalachian Dulcimers* (1983).

The more modern purpose-built version of the instrument was developed by Kenneth Bloom of Pilot Mountain, North Carolina in the late 1990s.[[1]](#cite_note-BarrettStauffer2012-1)
